title: [Enhancement] Engine should generate unique order IDs internally
body:
## Problem

Order IDs are currently provided by the caller as plain strings. This puts the burden of uniqueness on the user and opens the door to duplicate ID bugs (see related issue).

There is no built-in ID generation, sequencing, or validation.

## Proposed Solution

Add an internal order ID generator using either:
- Atomic counter: `atomic.AddUint64(&e.nextID, 1)` — simple, fast, monotonically increasing
- UUID: `google/uuid` — globally unique but heavier

The engine should assign IDs internally and return them to the caller:

```go
func (e *Engine) SubmitOrder(symbol string, req OrderRequest) (string, []model.Trade, error) {
    id := e.generateID()
    order := &model.Order{ID: id, ...}
    // ...
    return id, trades, nil
}
```

This also enables the engine to maintain an order index (`map[string]*Order`) for O(1) lookups.

## Priority
Medium — improves API safety and enables other optimizations.
